verdict: Great rhythmic mapping and standard streams, but severely marred by physically impossible 16th-note runs and jumps during active holds.

issues.problem: Holding U while playing a rapid 16th-note run (D, R, L) forces the player to perform an impossible one-foot stream on a dance pad.
The JUMP DR at 74.904 occurs while the L panel is held, creating an unintentional 3-panel 'hand' or forcing a highly awkward bracket release.
Holding L while executing a 16th-note stream of U, D, R, U requires the right foot to move at impossible speeds to hit all three remaining panels.
